name: Circular del Val de Zafán y Río Martín
name_original: Vía Verde del Val de Zafán — circular desde Samper de Calanda
description: Hike the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán circular route from Samper de Calanda, a moderate 7.3-mile trail with railway history and diverse views.
region_intro: Hiking around Samper De Calanda offers exploration of diverse Aragonese landscapes, characterized by a blend of dry plains and rolling hills. The region is shaped by the Ebro River basin and the Martín River, which supports both agriculture and local wildlife. Hikers can experience the fertile Vega del Martín, an irrigated plain, and areas featuring a unique steppe environment. Best hiking trails around Samper De Calanda The most popular hiking route is Laguna salada de Chiprana — circular, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete, offering views of a salt lake environment. Another top favourite among local hikers is Circular del Val de Zafán y Río Martín, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.8 km) path that follows the Martín River through varied terrain. Local hikers also love the Camí de Sant Jaume and GR 99 Loop from Rueda Monastery, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail leading through historical routes, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Hiking around Samper De Calanda is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and steppe landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. 5 hikers have used komoot to explore Samper De Calanda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Samper De Calanda?
A: Hiking in Samper De Calanda offers a diverse experience, characterized by a blend of dry plains and rolling hills. The region is significantly shaped by the Ebro River basin and the Martín River, which supports both agriculture and local wildlife. You can explore the fertile Vega del Martín, an irrigated plain, and areas featuring a unique steppe environment, providing varied scenery for your walks.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Samper De Calanda?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes to enjoy. For an easy walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167253173/laguna-salada-de-chiprana-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna salada de Chiprana — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.5 km long and offers views of a salt lake environment. A more moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167362799/circular-del-val-de-zafan-y-rio-martin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular del Val de Zafán y Río Martín&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.8 km path that follows the Martín River through varied terrain.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I discover along the trails?
A: The area around Samper De Calanda is rich in points of interest. You can find the 19th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5025865" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Torica Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;, an interesting historical monument. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6212554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martín River and its surroundings&lt;/a&gt; are also a significant natural feature. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167308240/cami-de-sant-jaume-and-gr-99-loop-from-rueda-monastery" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí de Sant Jaume and GR 99 Loop from Rueda Monastery&lt;/a&gt; will lead you through historical routes.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hiking options?
A: Absolutely. Two of the routes are rated as easy. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167253173/laguna-salada-de-chiprana-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna salada de Chiprana — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a short 4.5 km trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167308240/cami-de-sant-jaume-and-gr-99-loop-from-rueda-monastery" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí de Sant Jaume and GR 99 Loop from Rueda Monastery&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.4 km path, both suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Samper De Calanda?
A: The region's climate, with its blend of dry plains and river valleys, suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for hiking. During these seasons, temperatures are generally milder, making for more comfortable conditions to explore the diverse landscapes without the intense heat of summer or the cooler, potentially wetter, conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails?
A: For families, the easier routes are generally the best choice. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167253173/laguna-salada-de-chiprana-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna salada de Chiprana — circular&lt;/a&gt;, at 4.5 km, is a good option for a shorter outing. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167308240/cami-de-sant-jaume-and-gr-99-loop-from-rueda-monastery" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí de Sant Jaume and GR 99 Loop from Rueda Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, at 7.4 km, is also rated easy and offers historical interest, which can engage older children.

Q: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the trails?
A: The terrain around Samper De Calanda is quite varied. You'll find paths through dry plains, rolling hills, and along river valleys, particularly near the Martín River. Some areas also feature a unique steppe environment. This diversity means you can expect a mix of relatively flat sections and gentle ascents and descents.

Q: How long do the hikes typically take?
A: The duration of hikes varies depending on the route and your pace. For example, the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167253173/laguna-salada-de-chiprana-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna salada de Chiprana — circular&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour. A moderate trail like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167362799/circular-del-val-de-zafan-y-rio-martin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular del Val de Zafán y Río Martín&lt;/a&gt;, which is longer, typically takes around three hours.

Q: Are there any specific natural features to look out for?
A: Beyond the general landscapes, the Martín River is a prominent natural feature, shaping the local environment and supporting wildlife. The fertile Vega del Martín, an irrigated plain, showcases traditional agriculture, and near the Santa Quiteria area, you can find an explanatory panel highlighting the unique steppe environment. The salt lake environment of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167253173/laguna-salada-de-chiprana-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna salada de Chiprana&lt;/a&gt; is also a distinct natural attraction.

Q: Where can I find more information about the broader hiking network in the region?
A: While Samper De Calanda offers specific trails, the broader Bajo Aragón region provides an extensive network of both short (PR) and long-distance (GR) hiking trails. For more information on these regional routes, you can visit &lt;a href="https://visitbajoaragon.com/en/routes/hiking" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitbajoaragon.com&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the difficulty level of the hikes around Samper De Calanda?
A: The hiking trails around Samper De Calanda cater to various ability levels. You'll find a mix of easy walks, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167308240/cami-de-sant-jaume-and-gr-99-loop-from-rueda-monastery" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí de Sant Jaume and GR 99 Loop from Rueda Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, and moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167362799/circular-del-val-de-zafan-y-rio-martin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular del Val de Zafán y Río Martín&lt;/a&gt;. There are no difficult-rated trails listed, making the area accessible for most hikers.
tips: It is accessed by paths from the banks of the Martín, with fabulous views of the banks and the town of Samper de Calanda.

Beautiful greenway that was used in the past and is now a common route that connects the best-known towns in the area. Along the way you can see a set of bridges and tunnels that cross on both sides of the land that passes through it.

The relief of the municipality is characterized by the so-called Calanda Desert, an arid area with little rainfall, only softened by the presence of the Martín River, which crosses the territory to the west.
The Hermitage of Santa Quiteria is located towards the train station and to the left, you arrive at the hermitage, where water miraculously flows in such an arid land.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán — circular desde Samper de Calanda trail?
faqs.answer: This circular route is classified as a moderate hike. It requires good physical fitness and steady footing. While most paths are accessible, some sections may demand careful steps.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows a former railway line, which means you'll encounter generally gentle gradients without significant altitude changes. The path features several historic railway structures, including viaducts and tunnels. You'll walk through diverse landscapes, from open agricultural fields with olive and almond trees to impressive rock formations and Mediterranean forests. Expect to see deep cuts in reddish rock, adding to the visual interest.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán — circular desde Samper de Calanda?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in historical railway infrastructure and natural beauty. You can see the imposing "Puente de la Torica" viaduct, pass through the "Túnel del Montecico," and cross the Martín River via the "Puente Negro" or "Puente de San Carlos" viaduct. Don't miss the View of the Railway Viaduct and Samper de Calanda, and the historic Val de Zafán Greenway itself. The Hermitage of Santa Quiteria is also a notable stop.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and some sections requiring careful footing, it's best suited for families with older children who have good physical fitness and are accustomed to hiking. The gentle gradients of the former railway line make it generally accessible, but supervision is advised in certain areas.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife, and to ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during most seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended, and winter can be chilly but often offers clear views. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Samper de Calanda?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, Samper de Calanda serves as the starting and ending point for this circular route. You should be able to find suitable parking within or on the outskirts of the town, close to the Vía Verde access points. Look for designated parking areas or inquire locally upon arrival.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to hike this Vía Verde?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to access and hike along the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán. It's a public recreational path open to everyone.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that are part of the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán, the Gran travesía del Bajo Martín, and the Loma del Regadío y ribera del Martín.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the trail starts and ends in Samper de Calanda, you will find services, including cafes and restaurants, within the town itself. It's a good idea to plan your meals or refreshments before or after your hike in Samper de Calanda.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, appropriate footwear for varied terrain, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for the season. A camera is also a good idea to capture the scenic views and historical structures.

waypoints.name: View of the Railway Viaduct and Samper de Calanda
